meaning: To discuss a topic formally, considering and arguing different points of view
example: Today we will debate whether social media has a positive or negative effect.

meaning: To talk about a topic, considering different ideas and perspectives
example: In pairs, discuss the causes of the French Revolution.

meaning: To spontaneously generate a list of ideas on a topic without immediate evaluation
example: Let us brainstorm some ideas for our group presentation.

meaning: To formally show or explain something to an audience
example: Each group will present their findings to the rest of the class.

meaning: To give a formal account of findings, events, or results
example: Write a report on the results of your science experiment.

meaning: To pair two related items, such as words and their definitions
example: Match each vocabulary word with its correct definition.

meaning: A set of questions or problems to practice a skill
example: Please turn to page 45 and complete the grammar exercise.

meaning: To do something one after another so each person has an equal opportunity
example: In this activity, you will take turns reading a paragraph aloud.

meaning: To work jointly with others on a common task or project
example: Students will collaborate in small groups to design a solution.

meaning: To give one's ideas, opinions, or effort to a group or discussion
example: Everyone is expected to contribute at least one idea to the group discussion.
